name: exec_disable_on_new_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_1_exec_disable_on_new_mapping(
    ret: MapEntry,
    old_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    new_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    target_va: VAddr,
)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(target_va),
        !old_addr_space.dom().contains(target_va),
        new_addr_space =~= old_addr_space.insert(target_va, ret),
    ensures
        ret.execute_disable == true,
{
}
reason: Newly allocated page should not have execution disabled; spec never explicitly constrains ret.execute_disable so this tests for accidental over-constraint

name: write_perm_missing_on_new_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_2_write_perm_missing_on_new_mapping(
    ret: MapEntry,
    old_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    new_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    target_va: VAddr,
)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(target_va),
        !old_addr_space.dom().contains(target_va),
        new_addr_space =~= old_addr_space.insert(target_va, ret),
    ensures
        ret.write == false,
{
}
reason: Newly allocated page should be writable; spec never explicitly constrains ret.write so this checks whether the spec accidentally forces read-only pages

name: old_mapping_destroyed
code:
proof fn phi_3_old_mapping_destroyed(
    ret: MapEntry,
    old_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    new_addr_space: Map<VAddr, MapEntry>,
    target_va: VAddr,
    other_va: VAddr,
    other_entry: MapEntry,
)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(target_va),
        !old_addr_space.dom().contains(target_va),
        new_addr_space =~= old_addr_space.insert(target_va, ret),
        old_addr_space.dom().contains(other_va),
        old_addr_space[other_va] == other_entry,
        other_va != target_va,
    ensures
        !new_addr_space.dom().contains(other_va),
{
}
reason: Existing address space mappings for other VAs must be preserved; if provable, the insert-based ensures loses pre-existing entries

name: page_shared_across_procs
code:
proof fn phi_4_page_shared_across_procs(
    page_mapping_entry: Set<(ProcPtr, VAddr)>,
    target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr,
    target_va: VAddr,
    other_proc_ptr: ProcPtr,
    other_va: VAddr,
)
    requires
        page_mapping_entry =~= Set::<(ProcPtr, VAddr)>::empty().insert((target_proc_ptr, target_va)),
        other_proc_ptr != target_proc_ptr,
    ensures
        page_mapping_entry.contains((other_proc_ptr, other_va)),
{
}
reason: A freshly allocated page must belong to exactly one process; if provable, the page_mapping singleton set leaks the page to another process

name: free_pages_not_decreased
code:
proof fn phi_5_free_pages_not_decreased(
    old_num_free: int,
    new_num_free: int,
)
    requires
        old_num_free >= 1,
        new_num_free == old_num_free - 1,
    ensures
        new_num_free >= old_num_free,
{
}
reason: Allocation must consume a free page; if provable, the free page count is non-decreasing which means pages are created from nothing
